mechanism_narrative: Declines in the federal funds rate reduce corporate borrowing costs and compress equity discount rates, directly increasing the present value of expected cash flows and generating positive S&P 500 forward returns.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.02 over any 24-month rolling window, or 20-day forward returns show no statistical significance (p > 0.10) during rate decline periods.

mechanism_narrative: When the unemployment rate exceeds 6.0 percent, recession risk premiums surge and consumer spending forecasts deteriorate, causing institutional investors to de-risk equity portfolios and driving negative S&P 500 forward returns.
failure_condition: S&P 500 produces positive 20-day returns in more than 55 percent of instances where UNRATE exceeds 6.0 percent, or the signal loses predictive power (Rank IC < 0.01) in out-of-sample testing.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous expansion in retail sales and housing starts confirms broad-based consumer demand strength, which translates into higher corporate revenue growth expectations and generates positive S&P 500 forward returns over a quarterly horizon.
failure_condition: The joint signal fails to produce a positive mean return differential of at least 0.5 percent over 60 trading days, or the interaction term loses statistical significance (p > 0.05) in multivariate regression.

mechanism_narrative: Rising industrial production indices signal immediate capacity utilization improvements and supply-side momentum, which triggers short-term algorithmic buying and generates positive S&P 500 forward returns within a one-week window.
failure_condition: 5-day forward returns are negative or flat during periods of rising INDPRO, or the signal predictive accuracy drops below 52 percent in a walk-forward validation framework.

mechanism_narrative: Increases in total nonfarm payrolls directly boost aggregate wage income and consumer purchasing power, creating immediate positive sentiment shifts that drive upward S&P 500 price momentum over the subsequent five trading days.
failure_condition: The correlation between monthly payroll growth and 5-day forward returns remains below 0.05, or the strategy yields negative alpha after transaction costs in a live simulation.

mechanism_narrative: When the CPIAUCSL index surpasses 220.0, persistent inflation expectations force central banks to maintain restrictive policy, which compresses equity valuations and produces negative S&P 500 forward returns over a three-month horizon.
failure_condition: S&P 500 delivers positive 60-day returns in over 60 percent of months where CPIAUCSL exceeds 220.0, or the inflation threshold fails to separate return distributions at the 95 percent confidence level.

mechanism_narrative: Concurrent growth in real GDP and retail sales validates robust economic expansion, reducing default probabilities and boosting equity risk appetite, which generates positive S&P 500 forward returns over a one-month horizon.
failure_condition: The combined GDP and retail sales signal yields a negative mean return over 20 days, or the interaction effect is statistically indistinguishable from zero in a panel regression model.

mechanism_narrative: In a tight labor market, declining unemployment rates signal wage growth and strong corporate hiring, which supports earnings revisions and drives positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next twenty trading days.
failure_condition: Forward returns turn negative when UNRATE declines below 4.5 percent, or the signal information coefficient falls below 0.02 across multiple market cycles.
